question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $98,000 on December 2?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.content: Message Format ``` {"e": "continuous_kline",// Event type "E": 1607443058651,// Event time "ps": "BTCUSDT",// Pair "ct": "PERPETUAL"// Contract type "k":{"t": 1607443020000,// Kline start time "T": 1607443079999,// Kline close time "i": "1m",// Interval "f": 116467658886,// First trade ID "L": 116468012423,// Last trade ID "o":"18787.00",// Open price "c":"18804.04",// Close price "h":"18804.04",// High price "l":"18786.54",// Low price "v":"197.664",// volume "n": 543,// Number of trades "x": false,// Is this kline closed ? "q":"3715253.19494",// Quote asset volume "V":"184.769",// Taker buy volume "Q":"3472925.84746",// Taker buy quote asset volume "B": "0"// Ignore}}< pair> _< contractType> @continuousKline_< interval> ``` [...] Klines are uniquely identified by their open & close time. Parameters: : symbol (str) – STRING - Required - Trading symbol interval (str) – ENUM - Required - Kline interval startTime (int) – INT - Optional - Start time in milliseconds endTime (int) – INT - Optional - End time in milliseconds timeZone (str) – STRING - Optional - Default: 0 (UTC) limit (int) – INT - Optional - Default 500; max 1000 Supported kline intervals: : seconds: 1s minutes: 1m, 3...

evidences.title: Bitcoin's Price History
evidences.content: ### Late 2024 On Nov. 7, 2024, Bitcoin's price hit another all-time high of $75,902 following Donald Trump's re-election, a 9% increase from the closing price on Nov. 5. On Nov. 10, 2024, Bitcoin reached another milestone, crossing the $80,000 threshold.1 On Nov. 11, it continued pushing boundaries, and two days later, it closed at $90,574.1 Market exuberance continued apace in the following weeks, pushing Bitcoin to remarkable highs of $99,817 on Oanda, $99,860 on Coinbase, and $99,852 on Gemini on Nov. 22, 2024, by midday.8 9 10 On Dec. 8, 2024, Bitcoin broke through $100,000 and closed at $101,237.1 [...] By the summer of 2021, however, prices were down by 50%, closing at $30,838 on July 19. September saw another bull run, with BTC scraping $52,660, but a significant drawdown led it to close at $42,200 about two weeks later.1 On Nov. 8, 2021, Bitcoin reached another all-time high of $67,549. In mid-December 2021, the asset fell to a close of $46,709. The price started fluctuating more as uncertainty about inflation and the emergence of a new variant of COVID-19, Omicron, continued to spook investors.1 2 Between January and May 2022, Bitcoin's price continued to gradually decl...

evidences.title: Crypto Market Flows | 20 November 2025
evidences.content: ### Custody & Infrastructure – TradFi On-Ramps Mature Traditional market infrastructure has taken another step toward full crypto integration: Major U.S. custodial banks have resumed or expanded institutional Bitcoin custody, supporting both direct holdings and ETF structures. These institutions operate with double-digit trillions in assets under custody. In Europe, leading market infrastructure providers are launching Bitcoin and Ethereum custody and settlement for institutional clients, integrating digital assets into the same plumbing that serves listed equities and fixed income. In the U.S., regulatory guidance has eased key frictions: banks now have clearer pathways to offer crypto custody and support certain stablecoin activities without punitive balance-sheet treatment. [...] Tokenised U.S. Treasury products now hold roughly $8–9bn in underlying assets, up from under $2bn in 2024 – more than a 4x increase in less than two years. The broader tokenised real-world asset (RWA) market is estimated at $30bn+, with Treasuries and private credit as the two largest categories. Flagship vehicles such as Ondo’s OUSG manage hundreds of millions of dollars each, offering 30-day yields...

evidences.title: November 2025: What It Takes to HODL - Grayscale Research
evidences.content: Although the outlook is uncertain, we believe the four-year cycle thesis will prove to be incorrect, and that Bitcoin’s price will potentially make new highs next year. First, unlike in past cycles, there was no parabolic price increase during this bull market that might suggest overshooting (Exhibit 3). Second, Bitcoin’s market structure has changed, with new capital largely coming through ETPs and digital asset treasuries (DATs) rather than retail exchanges. Third, as we discuss further below, the broad macro market backdrop still appears favorable for the crypto asset class. Exhibit 3: No parabolic price increase this cycle [...] At the same time, a variety of fund flow indicators point to still-tepid demand: futures open interest declined further in November, ETP flows were negative until late in the month, and there may have been more Bitcoin “OG” selling. For the latter, on-chain data showed another spike in Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) in late November (Exhibit 5). Coin Days Destroyed is calculated as the number of coins transacted multiplied by the number of days since they were last transacted — CDD therefore increases when many old coins move at the same time. Similar to...

evidences.title: November 2025 Cryptocurrency Market Report - Alpha Node Global
evidences.content: Bitcoin carried most of the narrative and most of the market cap loss. After setting new all time highs above $126,000 in October, the price slid into the $80,000 region by 21 November. That left Bitcoin roughly 20% to 30% below its peak and removed around $400B from its market value. Spot ETFs amplified the move with about $3.48B of net outflows from United States spot Bitcoin ETFs, the second largest monthly redemption since launch. Other flow analyses place combined outflows in a similar range, and one widely cited figure shows BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust suffering a single day outflow of roughly $523 M on 19 November. Bitcoin Price vs 200 Day Moving Averages Source: [...] November was the first major deleveraging stress test of this cycle, wiping more than $1 T from crypto market value, but core infrastructure, exchanges and stablecoins continued to function without systemic failure. Flows and leverage were central to the move, with roughly $3.5 B of outflows from United States spot Bitcoin ETFs, about $1.4 B from Ethereum ETFs, the first stablecoin market cap contraction in more than two years, and a sharp drop in perpetual futures open interest and elevated liquidati...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price - History, Chart, 5 Years, 10 Years, Halving
evidences.content: But the trip of Bitcoin has been far from consistent. Sharp variations have come from market corrections. Driven by rising institutional investment and inflation worries, Bitcoin peaked in 2021 at previously unheard-of levels—above $60,000. These benefits, meantime, come with more volatility and government scrutiny. Though still prone to swings, by 2022 Bitcoin's price had found a more consistent range. From its modest start to a worldwide financial asset, the development of cryptocurrencies and changing viewpoint on them are highlighted. The price of Bitcoin reflects larger changes in finance as well as in technology. ## What does recent Bitcoin price analysis suggest? [...] ## What is Bitcoin's all-time high price record? November 2021 saw Bitcoin's all-time high price record, around $69,000. This milestone captures the rising institutional interest in blockchain technology as well as their acceptance. But Bitcoin's volatility means its price might vary greatly, hence investors have to exercise great caution. The price swings of Bitcoin rely on several elements, including market mood, laws, and more general state of the economy. Investing in Bitcoin presents hazards even if it...

evidences.title: VanEck Mid-August 2025 Bitcoin ChainCheck
evidences.content: ## 1. Market Performance In early August, bitcoin (BTC) slid to $112K with 92% of onchain holdings still in profit, before rebounding to $124K on August 13 to notch a new all-time high above July’s $123,838. CME basis funding rates surged to 9%, the highest since February 2025, reflecting renewed speculative appetite. Exchange-traded products (ETPs) and Digital Asset Treasuries (DATs) purchases added +54K BTC and +72K BTC, respectively, in July. ### CME Bitcoin Futures: Annualized 3-Month Rolling Basis CME Bitcoin Futures: Annualized 3-Month Rolling Basis CME Bitcoin Futures: Annualized 3-Month Rolling Basis Source: Glassnode as of 8/13/2025. Past performance is no guarantee of future results. Not intended as a recommendation to buy or sell any securities named herein. [...] ## 6. Forward Risks & Watchpoints As autumn approaches, several intertwined risks and opportunities emerge. Large outstanding options positions increase the chance that even modest volatility could trigger amplified price swings via dealer hedging. For DATs, a prolonged low-volatility regime may limit capital-raising ability, driving further mNAV compression. In mining, operational execution and integration...

evidences.title: Revisiting the Bitcoin Basis: How Momentum & Sentiment Impact the ...
evidences.content: Unlike traditional commodities, where the basis reflects storage, financing, and transportation costs as well as convenience yields, Bitcoin’s basis is primarily driven by price momentum and sentiment dynamics. We proxy Bitcoin’s momentum using two standardized measures: the Z-score of the MACD line, capturing short-term trend intensity, and the 90-day Z-score of the rolling 30-day return, reflecting medium-term relative strength. As detailed in Appendices A and B, these indicators show strong correlations of 0.50 and 0.54, respectively, with the CME Bitcoin futures basis (see Appendix C), underscoring their explanatory power in capturing the behavioral drivers of crypto basis formation. Figure 7: Momentum and Basis (Source: CF Benchmarks, Bloomberg as of 9/30/2025) [...] Figure 4: CME Open Interest and Front Month Basis (Source: CF Benchmarks, Bloomberg as of 9/30/2025) [...] Figure 5: Levered Funds Positioning and Front Month Basis (Source: CF Benchmarks, Bloomberg as of 9/30/2025) The participation of institutional investors in basis trades contributes to improved market efficiency and liquidity.
